story.iWant: to convert my preset file to a different format
story.soThat: I can use my presets in different photography software

tasks.task: Create converter.js module with WASM conversion wrapper
Implement convertFile() function with Promise-based async conversion
Add output validation for all formats (NP3, XMP, lrtemplate)
Integrate with main.js event flow (convertRequest listener)
Create UI state management (converting, success, error)
Add CSS styling for conversion states
Add HTML elements for status display

criterion.title: Call WASM Convert Function
criterion.requirement: Listen for convertRequest event (dispatched by Story 2-5)
Extract source file data (from Story 2-2)
Extract source format (from Story 2-3)
Extract target format (from Story 2-5)
Call WASM convert(fileData, sourceFormat, targetFormat)
Handle async Promise (conversion may take 1-100ms)
criterion.test: Upload Classic Chrome.np3, select XMP, click Convert, verify convert() called with correct arguments

criterion.title: Display Conversion Status (Loading → Success/Error)
criterion.requirement: Before conversion: Button shows "Convert to [Format]" (enabled)
During conversion: Button shows "Converting..." (disabled), spinner icon
Success: Button shows "Converted!" (disabled), checkmark icon
Error: Button shows "Conversion Failed" (enabled for retry), error icon
criterion.test: Click Convert → button changes to "Converting..." with spinner → success shows "Converted!" with checkmark

criterion.title: Handle Conversion Success
criterion.requirement: Store converted file data in memory (Uint8Array)
Store converted file metadata (size, format)
Display success message: "✓ Conversion complete! Your [format] preset is ready."
Enable download button (Story 2-7 dependency)
Log conversion stats to console (size, time, format)
criterion.test: Convert NP3 → XMP, verify success message, console log, download button enabled, getConvertedFileData() returns Uint8Array

criterion.title: Handle Conversion Errors (User-Friendly)
criterion.requirement: Display user-friendly error messages (not technical jargon)
Show "Try Again" button (re-enable convert button)
Log technical error to console (for debugging)
Don't crash UI (error is recoverable)
Map technical errors to user messages: "NP3 magic bytes invalid" → "File appears corrupted or not a valid NP3 preset."
criterion.test: Upload corrupted NP3 file, click Convert, verify user-friendly error displayed, console shows technical details, retry works

criterion.title: Performance Target (<100ms P95)
criterion.requirement: Measure conversion time (performance.now())
95% of conversions complete <100ms
Display conversion time in console log
No browser UI blocking during conversion
criterion.test: Convert 20 different files, record times, verify P95 <100ms, average <50ms, browser remains responsive

criterion.title: Memory Management (Clear Old Conversions)
criterion.requirement: Clear previous converted data when new conversion starts
Release Uint8Array references (avoid memory leak)
Handle rapid conversions (debounce if needed)
criterion.test: Upload file → convert to XMP → upload same file → convert to lrtemplate → verify only most recent stored, memory stable

criterion.title: Conversion Validation (Verify Output)
criterion.requirement: After conversion, verify output is valid format
NP3: Starts with "NCP" magic bytes (0x4E 0x43 0x50)
XMP: Valid XML structure (<?xml, crs: namespace)
lrtemplate: Valid Lua syntax (s = {)
If validation fails, treat as conversion error
Log validation result to console
criterion.test: Convert NP3 → XMP, verify output starts with "<?xml", console shows "Validation: XMP output valid"

criterion.title: Disable Multiple Concurrent Conversions
criterion.requirement: If conversion is in progress, disable Convert button
Ignore additional button clicks during conversion
Only one conversion can run at a time
criterion.test: Click Convert button, immediately click again while running, verify second click ignored, only one conversion runs

artifact.title: Technical Specification: Epic 2 - Web Interface
artifact.snippet: CRITICAL PATH story. Performs conversion using WASM module. Deliverables: Call convert(bytes, fromFormat, toFormat), progress indicator, error handling. Performance target: <100ms P95. Risk mitigation: Epic 1's 200-3500x performance buffer.

artifact.title: Technical Specification: Epic 2 - Web Interface
artifact.snippet: Signature: convert(inputBytes: Uint8Array, fromFormat: string, toFormat: string): Promise<Uint8Array>. Async, returns Promise. Auto-detection: fromFormat = "" triggers auto-detect. Thread-safe, multiple conversions can run concurrently.

artifact.title: Technical Specification: Epic 2 - Web Interface
artifact.snippet: WASM Target: <100ms for conversion (XMP parse 5-50ms, XMP generate 1-20ms). Epic 1 is 200-3500x faster than targets, providing cushion for WASM overhead. User perception: <100ms feels instant.

artifact.title: Product Requirements Document
artifact.snippet: One-click "Convert" button. Instant download of converted file. Clear error messages for invalid files. Success criteria: Conversion works reliably (100% success rate on valid files), Fast performance (<100ms for typical presets), Clear error messages, Validated output.

artifact.title: Architecture
artifact.snippet: Exposes: convert(), detectFormat(), getVersion(). Thread-safe, stateless. Binary size: 1.03MB compressed. Epic 1 conversion engine (no changes required). Handles async Promise patterns. Converts File → Uint8Array → Go []byte.

artifact.title: Story 2-2: File Upload Handling
artifact.snippet: Provides getCurrentFileData() and getCurrentFileName() functions. File data stored as Uint8Array in memory. Maximum file size: 10MB. FileReader API converts File to ArrayBuffer to Uint8Array.

artifact.title: Story 2-3: Format Detection
artifact.snippet: Dispatches formatDetected event with detected format. Uses WASM detectFormat() function. Detection time: <100ms, typically 1-5ms. Returns "np3" | "xmp" | "lrtemplate".

artifact.title: Story 2-5: Target Format Selection
artifact.snippet: Dispatches convertRequest event with {fromFormat, toFormat} on button click. Provides getSourceFormat() and getTargetFormat() functions. Prevents same-format conversion. Smart defaults: XMP→NP3, NP3→XMP.

artifact.reason: WASM convert() function exposed to JavaScript. Returns Promise<Uint8Array>. Calls converter.Convert(inputBytes, fromFormat, toFormat). Handles errors and Uint8Array conversion.
Registers WASM functions to JavaScript global scope. Sets up convert, detectFormat, extractParameters, getVersion functions.
Core conversion logic shared by all interfaces. Signature: Convert(input []byte, fromFormat, toFormat string) ([]byte, error). Stateless, thread-safe.
WASM loading and initialization. Creates Go instance, loads recipe.wasm, verifies functions available. Must complete before conversion can start.
Provides file data as Uint8Array for conversion. Story 2-2 dependency. Returns null if no file uploaded.
Provides original filename for output naming. Story 2-2 dependency. Used to generate converted filename with correct extension.
Story 2-3 format detection. Uses WASM detectFormat(). Returns detected format for source format parameter.
Story 2-5 dependency. Returns currently detected source format for conversion.
Story 2-5 dependency. Returns user-selected target format for conversion.
Central event handler. Will integrate converter.js convertFile() function. Coordinates file data, formats, UI updates.
Convert button element. Needs state updates (converting, success, error). Target for enabling/disabling during conversion.
Button styling. Needs additional states: .converting (gray, spinner), .success (green, checkmark), .error (red).

constraints.constraint: P95 conversion time must be <100ms (target based on Epic 1's 200-3500x performance buffer)
Browser UI must remain responsive during conversion (use async/await, not synchronous calls)
Only one converted file in memory at a time (clear previous on new conversion)
Maximum file size: 10MB (inherited from Story 2-2 file upload handling)
All conversion logic in WASM module - JavaScript only handles UI and data marshaling
Stateless conversion - no session storage, no localStorage for converted data
Event-driven integration - listen for convertRequest, dispatch conversionComplete
User-friendly error messages only (no technical jargon, stack traces to console only)
Must validate output format after conversion (magic bytes for NP3, XML declaration for XMP, Lua syntax for lrtemplate)
Prevent multiple concurrent conversions (disable button, use isConverting flag)
All processing client-side - no network requests during conversion
Must work in Chrome, Firefox, Safari (latest 2 versions each)

interface.name: WASM convert() function
interface.signature: convert(inputBytes: Uint8Array, fromFormat: string, toFormat: string): Promise<Uint8Array>
interface.description: Main WASM conversion function. Returns Promise that resolves to converted file data. Rejects on error with string message.

interface.name: WASM detectFormat() function
interface.description: Format detection function. Returns "np3", "xmp", or "lrtemplate". Used by Story 2-3.

interface.name: convertRequest event
interface.description: Dispatched by Story 2-5 when user clicks Convert button. Triggers this story's conversion logic.

interface.name: conversionComplete event
interface.description: Dispatched after successful conversion. Story 2-7 (download) will listen for this event.

interface.name: getCurrentFileData()
interface.description: Returns current file data for conversion. Story 2-2 dependency.

interface.name: getCurrentFileName()
interface.description: Returns current filename for output naming. Story 2-2 dependency.

interface.name: getSourceFormat()
interface.description: Returns detected source format. Story 2-5 dependency.

interface.name: getTargetFormat()
interface.description: Returns user-selected target format. Story 2-5 dependency.

standards.standard: Manual testing in browser DevTools (Chrome, Firefox, Safari)
Console logging for performance metrics (conversion time, file size)
Visual verification of UI states (converting, success, error)
Performance benchmarking with 20+ sample files (measure P50, P95, P99)
Memory profiling with DevTools (verify no memory leaks after 10 conversions)
Network monitoring with DevTools (verify zero requests during conversion)

locations.location: web/static/converter.js (unit tests can be added inline or separate test file)

ideas.idea: Test convertRequest event listener: Mock event dispatch, verify convert() called with correct parameters
Test UI state transitions: Verify button text/class changes for each state (converting, success, error)
Test success flow: Convert NP3→XMP, verify converted data stored, success message shown, download button enabled
Test error handling: Upload corrupted file (invalid magic bytes), verify user-friendly error shown, console has technical details
Test performance: Convert 20 files, log times, calculate P50/P95/P99, verify P95 <100ms
Test memory management: Convert 10 files in succession, take heap snapshots, verify memory stable
Test validation: Convert to each format, verify output headers correct (NCP for np3, <?xml for xmp, s = { for lrtemplate)
Test concurrency prevention: Rapid-click convert button, verify only one conversion runs, second click ignored
Test all 9 conversion combinations: np3→xmp, np3→lrtemplate, xmp→np3, xmp→lrtemplate, lrtemplate→np3, lrtemplate→xmp, plus 3 same-format rejections
Test with sample files from testdata/: Use existing 1,479 validated files for comprehensive testing
Test WASM not loaded: Mock scenario where convert() function undefined, verify graceful error
Test browser compatibility: Run same tests in Chrome, Firefox, Safari, verify identical behavior
